text:
Thing that pisses me off about technology these days is that there’s always an Update!

Update Update Update!

You can’t go a day without an Update!

Between the time you use an app and the Next time you use that app there WILL be an update that you HAVE to download!

Did they even Change Anything?
Feel the same way myself.
Ok Boomer
They probably did change something - they probably made it worse!

My favorite thing about new updates is half the time the update is like, “changed the UX to be worse than it was!” and the other half the time it’s “fixed security vulnerability that allowed hackers to instantly steal all of your money”. Isn’t technology wonderful?
Yeah, making things that much more obsolete so that you need to buy a replacment

Some of these companies need to be like Valve

Give us a Jungle Inferno update, and then go silent for 10+ years 😭✌️
You can share 10 photos instead of 4 in the new Bluesky update!
Hackers have started spoofing fake updates cuz people have gotten so complacent with daily updates
Yes, they did change something! They moved the button you're used to using the most 3 pixels to the left to throw off your muscle memory.
But how are we going to get on the ground market research from small funds
No AUM, not a fund
Citrini is just research?
Wait thats the name isn’t it
There are actual funds with “research” in the name, but yes
Claude compounding company
Good point. Upon further review, small ships, be careful but please post through it.
Get back to shore safely, but send the junior analyst back out again.
Data is a bit stale, no?
(and skimpy)
No, trackaipac just makes shit up. Opensecrets gets its data from the FEC.
Only thing stale here is your antisemitism

if it's for science, sure

NEW YORK (AP)—Another sell-off for artificial-intelligence stocks dragged the U.S. market sharply lower. The S&P 500 dropped 1.6% Wednesday for its first back-to-back drop in three weeks and is back to where it was in early May.

The Dow Jones Industrial Average tumbled 953 points, and the Nasdaq composite led the market lower with a 2% slide. Wall Street has been shaky since last week, when AI stocks went from roaring to records to suddenly turning lower.
Among the worries is that their prices have simply shot too high, too fast. Oil prices rose after President Donald Trump threatened more strikes on Iran.

Good one. Milton had the economic framework of CEOs only responsibility being to stock gains & laws against fraud. No social responsibility. Reagan implemented that philosophy through tax cuts & deregulation. Where this falls apart is corporate greed is limitless & humans have feelings & choices.

To quote Krugman, corporations used to have stakeholders[: employees, the country, and the shareholders.] Now, it's the shareholders. Yet the job creators meme persists. Jobs are costs, cutting them boosts profits, CEO gets raise.

Add that AI & robots are expected to take a huge % of jobs. That will also take a huge percent of SS, Medicare & unemployment taxes.
Employers will make more $$. 50-75mn Americans may be unemployed. We have to make up for that. Most fair way is by corporations & the richest paying a lot more.

scrap that project, and go with a new game while keeping the 'base concept' intact, which is where a realm reborn comes from. final fantasy 13 just happens to share UI design with final fantasy games of it's time period (9, 10, 11, 12, and 14) (2/2)

Since ID verification began its creep across the internet, social media platforms and their verification vendors have been collecting the sensitive data of its users, and then promptly mishandling or abusing said data for profit, surveillance or both.
Discord's support vendor Zendesk was compromised just a few months after OSA took effect, exposing the IDs of at least 70,000 users:

Then less than 2 weeks after announcing its teen-by-default rollout, Discord's UK ID verification vendor Persona was found to be running a mass surveillance scheme in collaboration with the US government behind the scenes.

It's puzzling how this never made headlines worldwide!

Tech companies are lobbying heavily for device/OS level ID verification. By linking your most personal details to their advertising profiles of you, they could sell these profiles for far more money, plus it relieves them of the cost and effort of carrying it out themselves.

Meta is the biggest lobbyist behind the push for ID verification.
See how deep the well goes here:

We all know that preventing children from creating or accessing explicit content is obviously the good thing to do.
And we also know that the excuse of 'think of the children' is all too often used to push for the violation of basic civil liberties. This case is no different.

For purposes of detecting such content, the government is demanding that tech companies run tamper-proof software on ALL smartphones and tablets in the UK to detect and remove explicit content before a child even sees it.

Cannabis Autos Created By This and Williams Wonder? Ruderalis?🤔

Mexican Rudy is widely believed to be an experimental 1970s cross between Russian Cannabis ruderalis and a Mexican sativa, potentially originating from government research at the University of Mississippi or the University of Ottawa.

I call bs on the Mexican Rudy Russian Cannabis Ruderalis connection.

After researching for awhile (I unfortunately was not there!), I'm going to have to agree with Todd McCormick about his recessive hemp trait claim and that it's likely that ruderalis was not used in the creation of autos at all.
It took more than five generations of crossing those together to get some type of stabilization of getting the recessive trait and some form of noticeable potency from the flower.
But... what are your thoughts on it?

Coal externalities in terms of non-climate impacts substantial and more localised (acid rain, particulate pollution). But cost of unusually expensive household and industrial energy prices is also very high and seems (if environmentalists are right) to be due in part to the absence of coal

Yes and there might be positive externalities from being in the clean energy vanguard. Leaving aside the ethical question of whether a rich country should go back to coal, do we know how much higher UK energy costs are by not using coal, and size of the externalities of either choice?

I don't think we do know, but then it's not an option I've seen much discussed. Given the scale of the issues we have with our energy policy I do find it surprising there's so little debate and information about what we are doing and could do
Agreed.
Is the idea that the government would invest in and own the coal mines? Or private companies?

There probably have been some positive benefits from leading on wind as we have some manufacturing from that, but not nearly as much as we should have - a real failing of the last government. Hopefully SMRs give us a chance to rebuild reasonably priced baseload and catalyse UK manufacturing
